sources.label: RetroAchievements — Pokémon Crystal (base set)

sources.label: RetroAchievements guides wiki — Pokémon Crystal
sources.note: Community guide (Set-mode gym tips, datetime/phone tricks). Note: uses older achievement names.

achievements.name: Chikorita, Cyndaquil or Totodile
achievements.callout_do: Choose your starter from Professor Elm.
howto: Chikorita, Cyndaquil or Totodile — you can only pick one, and you cannot trade for the others in a POC.

achievements.name: Bayleef, Quilava or Croconaw
achievements.callout_do: Evolve your starter to its second stage by leveling up.
howto: Chikorita → Bayleef at Lv. 16, Cyndaquil → Quilava at Lv. 14, Totodile → Croconaw at Lv. 18.

achievements.name: Meganium, Typhlosion or Feraligatr
achievements.description: Evolve your starter to its third form.
achievements.callout_do: Fully evolve your starter — this is required before Falkner.
howto: Bayleef → Meganium at Lv. 32, Quilava → Typhlosion at Lv. 36, Croconaw → Feraligatr at Lv. 30. Consider leaving it unevolved a while so it learns moves faster for the grind.

achievements.name: Pidgey
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Pidgey (Morning/Day).
howto: Fully evolving Pidgey is one of the bigger grinds before Falkner.

achievements.name: Pidgeotto
achievements.callout_do: Level Pidgey to Lv. 18 to get Pidgeotto.

achievements.name: Pidgeot
achievements.callout_do: Level Pidgeotto to Lv. 36 to get Pidgeot.

achievements.name: Hoppip
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Hoppip (Morning/Day).
howto: Hoppip only knows Tackle (from Lv. 10) as an attacking move, even unevolved up to Skiploom/Jumpluff — train it patiently.

achievements.name: Skiploom
achievements.callout_do: Level Hoppip to Lv. 18 to get Skiploom.

achievements.name: Jumpluff
achievements.callout_do: Level Skiploom to Lv. 27 to get Jumpluff.

achievements.name: Sentret
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Sentret (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Furret
achievements.callout_do: Level Sentret to Lv. 15 to get Furret.

achievements.name: Hoothoot
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Hoothoot (Night).

achievements.name: Noctowl
achievements.callout_do: Level Hoothoot to Lv. 20 to get Noctowl.

achievements.name: Rattata
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Rattata (any time).

achievements.name: Raticate
achievements.callout_do: Level Rattata to Lv. 20 to get Raticate.

achievements.name: Phanpy
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Phanpy (Morning).
howto: Phanpy has a 50% chance to flee each turn. If it wastes too many balls, come back later with a Gastly that knows Mean Look.

achievements.name: Donphan
achievements.callout_do: Level Phanpy to Lv. 25 to get Donphan.

achievements.name: Geodude
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Geodude (any time).

achievements.name: Graveler
achievements.callout_do: Level Geodude to Lv. 25 to get Graveler.
howto: Graveler's evolution into Golem is a trade evolution, so Golem is not obtainable in a POC.

achievements.name: Spearow
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Spearow (any time except Night).

achievements.name: Fearow
achievements.callout_do: Level Spearow to Lv. 20 to get Fearow.

achievements.name: Caterpie
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Caterpie (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Metapod
achievements.callout_do: Level Caterpie to Lv. 7 to get Metapod.

achievements.name: Butterfree
achievements.callout_do: Level Metapod to Lv. 10 to get Butterfree.
howto: Butterfree gives ridiculous EXP when flushed out of Headbutt trees later.

achievements.name: Weedle
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Weedle (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Kakuna
achievements.callout_do: Level Weedle to Lv. 7 to get Kakuna.

achievements.name: Beedrill
achievements.callout_do: Level Kakuna to Lv. 10 to get Beedrill.

achievements.name: Spinarak
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Spinarak (Night).

achievements.name: Ariados
achievements.callout_do: Level Spinarak to Lv. 22 to get Ariados.

achievements.name: Ledyba
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Ledyba (Morning).

achievements.name: Ledian
achievements.callout_do: Level Ledyba to Lv. 18 to get Ledian.

achievements.name: Zubat
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Zubat (Night).
howto: Keep Zubat in your party at all times: Golbat evolves into Crobat via high happiness, and leveling on the route you caught it gives more friendship per level in Crystal.

achievements.name: Golbat
achievements.callout_do: Level Zubat to Lv. 22 to get Golbat.

achievements.name: Crobat
achievements.callout_do: Raise Golbat's happiness (any time) to get Crobat.

achievements.name: Poliwag
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Poliwag (Night).

achievements.name: Poliwhirl
achievements.callout_do: Level Poliwag to Lv. 25 to get Poliwhirl.
howto: Poliwhirl needs a Water Stone for Poliwrath, which you cannot obtain yet — that comes later.

achievements.name: Bellsprout
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Bellsprout (any time) — and a second for the Onix trade.

achievements.name: Weepinbell
achievements.callout_do: Level Bellsprout to Lv. 21 to get Weepinbell.
howto: Weepinbell needs a Leaf Stone for Victreebel, handled later.

achievements.name: Gastly
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Gastly (Night) — on Route 31 or in Sprout Tower.

achievements.name: Haunter
achievements.callout_do: Level Gastly to Lv. 25 to get Haunter.
howto: Haunter's evolution into Gengar is a trade evolution, so Gengar is not obtainable in a POC. A Haunter with Mean Look/Night Shade helps catch roaming legendaries later.

achievements.name: Dunsparce
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Dunsparce (1% encounter — be patient).
howto: A Dunsparce with Rage is highly recommended for the Falkner Set challenge: it is Normal type and takes little damage.

achievements.name: Teddiursa
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Teddiursa (Morning).
howto: Another fleeing Pokémon — bring something to trap it if needed.

achievements.name: Ursaring
achievements.callout_do: Level Teddiursa to Lv. 30 to get Ursaring.

achievements.name: Onix
achievements.callout_do: Trade your spare Bellsprout for Onix.
howto: This in-game trade is the only way to get Onix this early.

achievements.name: Growlithe
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Growlithe (Morning/Day).
howto: Growlithe needs a Fire Stone for Arcanine, obtained later from a Pokégear contact.

achievements.name: Unown
achievements.callout_do: Catch an Unown (it may flee).
howto: Catch one now; you can collect all 26 forms later once every puzzle chamber is unlocked.

achievements.name: The Silver Case
achievements.description: Give your rival's name to the officer at Professor Elm's lab.
achievements.callout_do: Give your rival's name to the officer at Elm's lab.
howto: Story-related — happens automatically when you return from Mr. Pokémon.

achievements.name: Zephyr Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Falkner with all 46 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.
achievements.callout_do: POC checkpoint: have all 46 obtainable Pokémon registered before you beat Falkner.
howto: This subset milestone confirms the Part 1 dex is complete.

achievements.name: Flying in Father's Footsteps
achievements.description: Defeat Falkner in Violet City and earn the Zephyr Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Falkner for the Zephyr Badge.

achievements.name: Clipping the Pigeon's Wings
achievements.description: Defeat Falkner in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 9 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Falkner in Set style, Johto-only, all Pokémon ≤ Lv. 9, no Pack items.
howto: If you chose Cyndaquil or Totodile this is easy. Otherwise a Dunsparce from Dark Cave with Rage (more damage each time it is hit) shreds the gym; it is Normal type so takes little damage.
Set the battle style to Set and remove items from your bag's usable pool for the fight.

achievements.name: Togepi
achievements.callout_do: Hatch the Egg into Togepi by walking with it in your party.

achievements.name: Togetic
achievements.callout_do: Raise Togepi's happiness → Togetic.

achievements.name: But Which Came First?
achievements.description: Receive an Everstone from Professor Elm by showing him the hatched Mystery Egg.
achievements.callout_do: Show Elm your hatched Togepi for the Everstone.

achievements.name: Wooper
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Wooper (Night).

achievements.name: Quagsire
achievements.callout_do: Level Wooper to Lv. 20 → Quagsire.

achievements.name: Ekans
achievements.callout_do: Catch an Ekans (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Arbok
achievements.callout_do: Level Ekans to Lv. 22 → Arbok.

achievements.name: Magikarp
achievements.callout_do: Fish up a Magikarp (anywhere).
howto: Magikarp can't battle until it learns Tackle — train it once it can.

achievements.name: Gyarados
achievements.callout_do: Level Magikarp to Lv. 20 → Gyarados.

achievements.name: Tentacool
achievements.callout_do: Fish up a Tentacool (New Bark Town).

achievements.name: Tentacruel
achievements.callout_do: Level Tentacool to Lv. 30 → Tentacruel.

achievements.name: Krabby
achievements.callout_do: Fish up a Krabby (Cherrygrove City) — get a second for a trade.

achievements.name: Kingler
achievements.callout_do: Level Krabby to Lv. 28 → Kingler.

achievements.name: Goldeen
achievements.callout_do: Fish up a Goldeen (Dark Cave).

achievements.name: Seaking
achievements.callout_do: Level Goldeen to Lv. 33 → Seaking.

achievements.name: Qwilfish
achievements.callout_do: Fish up a Qwilfish (Route 32, during a swarm).
howto: Toggle daylight savings with Mom to force Ralph's swarm call.

achievements.name: Sandshrew
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Sandshrew (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Sandslash
achievements.callout_do: Level Sandshrew to Lv. 22 → Sandslash.

achievements.name: Criminal Underground
achievements.description: Defeat the Rocket Grunt leading the raid on the Slowpoke Well.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat the Rocket Grunt leading the Slowpoke Well raid.

achievements.name: Slowpoke
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Slowpoke.

achievements.name: Slowbro
achievements.callout_do: Level Slowpoke to Lv. 37 → Slowbro.
howto: Slowpoke's other evolution, Slowking, is a trade evolution — not obtainable in a POC.

achievements.name: Apri-Cornucopia
achievements.description: Receive a batch of at least 10 special Poké Balls at once from Kurt in Azalea Town.
achievements.callout_do: Receive at least 10 special Poké Balls at once from Kurt.
howto: Hand Kurt a batch of Apricorns so he returns 10+ balls in one go.

achievements.name: Oddish
achievements.callout_do: Catch an Oddish (Night).

achievements.name: Gloom
achievements.callout_do: Level Oddish to Lv. 21 → Gloom.
howto: Gloom needs a Leaf Stone or Sun Stone later for Vileplume/Bellossom.

achievements.name: Paras
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Paras (any time).

achievements.name: Parasect
achievements.callout_do: Level Paras to Lv. 24 → Parasect.

achievements.name: Psyduck
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Psyduck (Night).

achievements.name: Golduck
achievements.callout_do: Level Psyduck to Lv. 33 → Golduck.

achievements.name: Venonat
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Venonat (Night).

achievements.name: Venomoth
achievements.callout_do: Level Venonat to Lv. 31 → Venomoth.

achievements.name: I Slept Like a Log...
achievements.description: Encounter a sleeping Pokémon by using Headbutt on a tree.
achievements.callout_do: Headbutt a tree and run into a sleeping Pokémon.

achievements.name: Exeggcute
achievements.callout_do: Headbutt an Exeggcute out of a tree (Route 32).
howto: Exeggcute needs a Leaf Stone later for Exeggutor.

achievements.name: Pineco
achievements.callout_do: Headbutt a Pineco out of a tree (Ilex Forest).

achievements.name: Forretress
achievements.callout_do: Level Pineco to Lv. 31 → Forretress.

achievements.name: Aipom
achievements.callout_do: Headbutt an Aipom out of a tree (Azalea Town).

achievements.name: Heracross
achievements.callout_do: Headbutt a Heracross out of a tree (Azalea Town).
howto: Heracross gives huge EXP and is a great team member; not required for the Hive milestone but part of the dex.

achievements.name: Hive Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Bugsy with all 77 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex; Heracross is not required.
achievements.callout_do: POC checkpoint: 77 Pokémon registered before Bugsy (Heracross not required).

achievements.name: Entomology Prodigy
achievements.description: Defeat Bugsy in Azalea Town and earn the Hive Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Bugsy in Azalea Town for the Hive Badge.

achievements.name: Did Someone Call an Exterminator?
achievements.description: Defeat Bugsy in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 16 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Bugsy in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 16, no Pack items.
howto: A Dunsparce with Rage still shreds this gym; Cyndaquil starters win outright. Hoothoot/Wooper also help.

achievements.name: Drowzee
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Drowzee (Night).

achievements.name: Hypno
achievements.callout_do: Level Drowzee to Lv. 26 → Hypno.

achievements.name: Abra
achievements.description: Catch an Abra or receive one from the Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Catch an Abra (any time) — and a second for the Machop trade.

achievements.name: Kadabra
achievements.callout_do: Level Abra to Lv. 16 → Kadabra.
howto: Kadabra's evolution into Alakazam is a trade evolution — not obtainable.

achievements.name: Ditto
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Ditto (Route 34).

achievements.name: Snubbull
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Snubbull (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Granbull
achievements.callout_do: Level Snubbull to Lv. 23 → Granbull.

achievements.name: Jigglypuff
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Jigglypuff (Route 34).

achievements.name: Served Shiny Side Up?
achievements.description: Receive the Odd Egg from the Day-Care Man on Route 34.
achievements.callout_do: Receive the Odd Egg from the Day-Care Man on Route 34.

achievements.name: Tyrogue
achievements.description: Hatch a Tyrogue. (SAVE before you obtain the odd egg from the couple, read comments).
achievements.callout_do: Hatch the Odd Egg into Tyrogue (save before receiving the egg).
howto: With the exception of Igglybuff, the others aren't available yet — Tyrogue gives the most dex progress. There's a high shiny chance.

achievements.name: Hitmonchan
achievements.callout_do: Evolve Tyrogue at Lv. 20 with Defense > Attack → Hitmonchan.

achievements.name: Hitmonlee
achievements.callout_do: Evolve Tyrogue at Lv. 20 with Attack > Defense → Hitmonlee.

achievements.name: Hitmontop
achievements.callout_do: Evolve Tyrogue at Lv. 20 with Attack = Defense → Hitmontop.

achievements.name: Igglybuff
achievements.callout_do: Breed Jigglypuff with Ditto and hatch an Igglybuff.

achievements.name: Machop
achievements.callout_do: Trade your spare Abra for a Machop in the Underground.

achievements.name: Machoke
achievements.callout_do: Level Machop to Lv. 28 → Machoke.
howto: Machoke's evolution into Machamp is a trade evolution — not obtainable.

achievements.name: Cubone
achievements.description: Obtain a Cubone from the Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Buy a Cubone with coins at the Game Corner.

achievements.name: Marowak
achievements.callout_do: Level Cubone to Lv. 28 → Marowak.

achievements.name: Wobbuffet
achievements.description: Obtain a Wobbuffet from the Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Buy a Wobbuffet with coins at the Game Corner.

achievements.name: Deals on Wheels
achievements.description: Promote the Miracle Cycle bike shop in Goldenrod City and be allowed to keep the bike.
achievements.callout_do: Promote the Miracle Cycle bike shop so you keep the bike.

achievements.name: Our Store's Up High, But Our Prices Are Low
achievements.description: Make a purchase in the Rooftop Sale on the roof of the Goldenrod Department Store.
achievements.callout_do: Make a purchase at the Goldenrod Dept. Store Rooftop Sale.

achievements.name: Gambler's Charity
achievements.description: Learn a new move from Bill's father outside the Goldenrod City Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Learn a move from Bill's father outside the Game Corner.

achievements.name: Password Perfectionist
achievements.description: Add Buena's phone number to your Pokégear after earning 30 Blue Card points.
achievements.callout_do: Earn 30 Blue Card points to add Buena's number to your PokéGear.
howto: Listen to Buena's Password on the radio, then answer at the Goldenrod radio desk for points each day.

achievements.name: Megaton Chances
achievements.description: See Golem by lining up two 7s on a slot machine at a Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Line up two 7s on a Game Corner slot machine to see Golem.

achievements.name: Underage Gambling Pays Off!
achievements.description: Earn a 7-7-7 on a slot machine at a Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Hit a 7-7-7 on a Game Corner slot machine.

achievements.name: Double Dozen Deduction
achievements.description: Win a x24 bet in the Card Flip minigame at a Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Win a x24 bet in the Game Corner Card Flip minigame.

achievements.name: Yanma
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Yanma (1% encounter — be patient).

achievements.name: Nidoran F
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Nidoran♀ (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Nidorina
achievements.callout_do: Level Nidoran♀ to Lv. 16 → Nidorina.

achievements.name: Nidoran M
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Nidoran♂ (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Nidorino
achievements.callout_do: Level Nidoran♂ to Lv. 16 → Nidorino.

achievements.name: Sunkern
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Sunkern (daytime).

achievements.name: Scyther
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Scyther in the Bug-Catching Contest.

achievements.name: Pinsir
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Pinsir in the Bug-Catching Contest.

achievements.name: Cast Your Net Far & Wide
achievements.description: Win the Bug-Catching Contest at the National Park.
achievements.callout_do: Win the Bug-Catching Contest.
howto: Save before entering so you can retry. Put a Pokémon to sleep and catch it at full HP for the best score; a Scyther or Pinsir is ideal.

achievements.name: Cuddly Crawlie Challenge
achievements.description: Win the Bug-Catching Contest without catching a Butterfree, a Beedrill, a Scyther, or a Pinsir.
achievements.callout_do: Win the contest WITHOUT catching Butterfree, Beedrill, Scyther or Pinsir.

achievements.name: Earning Style Points
achievements.description: Win the Bug-Catching Contest with Cooltrainer Nick as a participant.
achievements.callout_do: Win the contest with Cooltrainer Nick as a participant.

achievements.name: Element of Generosity
achievements.description: Receive an Evolution stone from a registered Pokégear Trainer.
achievements.callout_do: Receive an evolution stone from a registered PokéGear Trainer.
howto: Gina gives Leaf Stones, Alan gives Fire Stones, plus later contacts for Water/Thunder Stones.

achievements.name: Wigglytuff
achievements.callout_do: Use a Moon Stone on Jigglypuff → Wigglytuff.
howto: You get three Moon Stones across Parts 3 and 5; spread them across Wigglytuff, Nidoking and Nidoqueen.

achievements.name: Nidoking
achievements.callout_do: Use a Moon Stone on Nidorino → Nidoking.

achievements.name: Nidoqueen
achievements.callout_do: Use a Moon Stone on Nidorina → Nidoqueen.

achievements.name: Sunflora
achievements.callout_do: Use a Sun Stone (Bug Contest prize) on Sunkern → Sunflora.

achievements.name: Bellossom
achievements.callout_do: Use a Sun Stone on Gloom → Bellossom.

achievements.name: Vileplume
achievements.callout_do: Use a Leaf Stone on Gloom → Vileplume.
howto: Catch/raise a second Gloom so you can get both Bellossom and Vileplume.

achievements.name: Exeggcutor
achievements.callout_do: Use a Leaf Stone on Exeggcute → Exeggutor.

achievements.name: Victreebel
achievements.callout_do: Use a Leaf Stone on Weepinbell → Victreebel.

achievements.name: Arcanine
achievements.callout_do: Use a Fire Stone (from Alan) on Growlithe → Arcanine.

achievements.name: Plain Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Whitney with all 109 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.
achievements.callout_do: POC checkpoint: 109 Pokémon registered before Whitney.

achievements.name: No Crying over Spilt Milk
achievements.description: Defeat Whitney in Goldenrod City and earn the Plain Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Whitney in Goldenrod City for the Plain Badge.
howto: Her Miltank is infamous — Dunsparce with Rage or a Heracross handle it.

achievements.name: Attack of the Mad Cow
achievements.description: Defeat Whitney in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 20 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Whitney in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 20, no Pack items.
howto: Lead with Dunsparce and spam Rage; even Miltank falls.

achievements.name: Falsa Wood
achievements.description: Catch the Sudowoodo on Route 36 and register it in the Pokédex.
achievements.callout_do: Catch the Sudowoodo on Route 36 and register it.

achievements.name: Sudowoodo
achievements.callout_do: Catch the Sudowoodo (save before using the SquirtBottle).

achievements.name: Stantler
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Stantler (Night).

achievements.name: Eevee
achievements.callout_do: Receive Eevee from Bill in Goldenrod.

achievements.name: Flareon
achievements.callout_do: Use a Fire Stone on Eevee → Flareon.

achievements.name: Jolteon
achievements.callout_do: Use a Thunder Stone on Eevee → Jolteon.
howto: Thunder Stones come from Lass Dana's PokéGear calls (Route 38).

achievements.name: Vaporeon
achievements.callout_do: Use a Water Stone on Eevee → Vaporeon.
howto: Water Stones come from Fisherman Tully's calls (Route 42 area).

achievements.name: Espeon
achievements.callout_do: Raise Eevee's happiness during the DAY → Espeon.

achievements.name: Umbreon
achievements.callout_do: Raise Eevee's happiness during the NIGHT → Umbreon.

achievements.description: Complete the Eusine sidequest in the Burned Tower.
achievements.callout_do: Complete the Eusine sidequest in the Burned Tower.

achievements.name: Koffing
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Koffing (Burned Tower).

achievements.name: Weezing
achievements.description: Evolve Koffing or catch a Weezing.
achievements.callout_do: Level Koffing to Lv. 35 → Weezing (or catch a Weezing).

achievements.name: Magnemite
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Magnemite (any time).

achievements.name: Magneton
achievements.callout_do: Level Magnemite to Lv. 30 → Magneton.
howto: Magneton's evolution into Magnezone doesn't exist in Gen 2.

achievements.name: Miltank
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Miltank (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Tauros
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Tauros (Morning/Day).

achievements.name: Meowth
achievements.callout_do: Catch a Meowth (Night).

achievements.name: Persian
achievements.callout_do: Level Meowth to Lv. 28 → Persian.

achievements.name: Udder Selflessness
achievements.description: Nurse Moomoo back to health at Moomoo Farm.
achievements.callout_do: Nurse Moomoo back to health at Moomoo Farm.

achievements.name: Voltorb
achievements.callout_do: Trade a Krabby for Voltorb in Olivine.

achievements.name: Electrode

chapters.title: Chapter 0 — The Basics (read first)
chapters.intro: A Professor Oak Challenge means catching and evolving as many Pokémon as possible before each gym badge becomes required to proceed (or before you need an HM that needs a badge). The dex comes first — your gym challenge goes on hold. This guide fuses Mewlax's POC route with both RetroAchievements sets: the base game set and the Professor Oak Challenge subset. Facts come straight from those sources; nothing is invented.

items.text: Catch and evolve as many Pokémon as is possible until a gym badge is required to proceed or you need a HM move that requires a badge. Where gyms can be done in any order, always get the badge that unlocks the most Pokémon (this guide picks the order for you).

You cannot trade with other versions. Trade evolutions are off the table, you get no version exclusives, and you are stuck with the decisions you make such as your starter. In-game trades ARE allowed.
Optional: call yourself 'Oak' or 'Prof. Oak' for fun.

Move sets are slow. Consider keeping three-stage Pokémon unevolved a little longer so they learn better moves, and keep multiple attacking moves over status moves like Growl or Sand Attack — you will KO huge numbers of wild Pokémon.

Time of day matters. Crystal pushes this further than Gold/Silver — almost every route has Pokémon that appear only in one or two slots. Morning is 4am–9:59am, Day is 10am–5:59pm, Night is 6pm–3:59am.
This is a very grindy challenge; the part before Falkner alone can take tens of hours. It gets less grindy as the game proceeds.
This guide lists the Pokémon you can catch at the earliest opportunity plus required items — it is not a full walkthrough of optimal EXP routes. Consult Bulbapedia for deeper detail.

sections.heading: The Moon Stone savings plan (start this immediately)

items.text: When you leave New Bark Town after delivering the egg to Professor Elm, speak to your Mother before leaving so she starts saving your money. A quarter of every trainer's winnings goes to her automatically; you can also deposit manually at home to speed it up.

Thresholds and the item she buys: P900 → Super Potion; P4,000 → Repel; P7,000 → Super Potion; P10,000 → Charmander Doll; P15,000 → Moon Stone. Because her savings drop each time she buys, you need P18,270 deposited in total to reach the Moon Stone.
The phone call that delivers an item only triggers when you defeat a trainer while she is already at the threshold, or the winnings from that battle push her over it.

Many evolution stones and swarm catches come from Pokégear contacts calling you. You can force a call: go to New Bark Town, talk to your mother, and toggle daylight savings (alternate winter/summer time) to instantly prompt a contact to call.

To change the in-game date/time directly, on the title screen press Down + Select + B together to reach the password prompt. The password is based on your name, ID and money, so it stays the same as long as your money doesn't change — handy for save-reset tricks.

Celebi is obtainable only on the Virtual Console release (via the GS Ball). Cartridge players cannot get it. Final dex totals: 206 on cartridge, 207 on Virtual Console. VC-only steps are flagged inline.

chapters.title: Chapter 1 — Pre-Badge #1: Falkner
chapters.intro: Everything you can do BEFORE the first badge from Falkner — the most daunting stretch of the challenge. Thankfully there is no Jigglypuff on Route 46, so no Moon Stone is needed yet (but get your Mother saving from the very start). By the end you should have caught 46 Pokémon, including a fully evolved starter and Pidgeot.

items.text: Set the clock and start the game. Your Mother gives you a PokéGear; head to Professor Elm's lab. He asks you to visit Mr. Pokémon and lets you take one Pokémon partner.

Pick one starter: Chikorita (→ Bayleef Lv. 16 → Meganium Lv. 32), Cyndaquil (→ Quilava Lv. 14 → Typhlosion Lv. 36), or Totodile (→ Croconaw Lv. 18 → Feraligatr Lv. 30). Chikorita is the weakest start given all the bug/flying/poison early on; Totodile fully evolves earliest at Lv. 30.

Visit Mr. Pokémon's house (north past Cherrygrove), then return to New Bark Town to receive your Pokédex and Poké Balls — now the challenge truly begins. Talk to Mom before leaving town to start the savings.
Straight out of New Bark Town. Pidgey, Hoppip and Sentret appear Morning/Day, Hoothoot at Night, and Rattata at all times.
Through the northern gate of Route 29 you reach the lower section of Route 46. Phanpy appears in the morning, Spearow any time except night, and Geodude at any time.
Pass through Cherrygrove City and head north to Route 30. Ledyba appears in the morning, Caterpie and Weedle Morning/Day, and Spinarak, Poliwag and Zubat at night.
Head further north onto Route 31. Bellsprout appears at all times and Gastly at night (or get one at night in Sprout Tower). Catch a SECOND Bellsprout for an upcoming trade.
Instead of heading west to Violet City, enter Dark Cave. You won't get far, but you can stumble far enough to catch a Teddiursa in the morning and a super-rare 1% Dunsparce.

It is not time for the gym yet. In one of the town houses, someone wants to trade an Onix for your Bellsprout — definitely do this, as Onix can't be caught until much later. Sprout Tower has a Gastly at night and the Flash HM at the top.
Head west out of Violet City. Exclusive to Crystal, a small patch of grass here holds a Growlithe during Morning/Day.
Go through the gate house south of the Route 36 grass to reach the Ruins of Alph. Solve the first tile puzzle to encounter Unown.

That's the end of catching for the first section. Grind your team up — Sprout Tower at night is great against Gastly, and Dark Cave / Route 31 / Ruins of Alph are good for the rest. The killers are the fully evolved starter, Pidgeot and Ursaring.
Back in New Bark Town, after the Mr. Pokémon errand, an officer at Professor Elm's lab asks for your rival's name (he raided the lab and stole a starter). Give it to register the achievement.
With all 46 Pokémon caught and your starter and Pidgey fully evolved, enter the Violet City Gym and challenge Falkner, who uses Flying types (Pidgey, Pidgeotto).

chapters.title: Chapter 2 — Pre-Badge #2: Bugsy
chapters.intro: With Falkner down, an aide hands you the Egg at the Violet City Pokémon Center — it hatches into Togepi. Work south through Route 32, the Old Rod fishing tour, Union Cave, Slowpoke Well and Ilex Forest, then start Headbutting trees. Target: 77 caught before Bugsy.

items.text: After beating Falkner, Professor Elm calls: an aide is at the Pokémon Center with a present — the Egg. Keep it in your party so its happiness rises while you grind; it hatches into Togepi.
Show the hatched Togepi to Professor Elm to receive an Everstone.
Head south from Violet City (skip Ruins of Alph for now). Ekans appears Morning/Day, Wooper only at Night. The Pokémon Center on this route has a man who gives you the Old Rod.
With the Old Rod, backtrack to fish up new Pokémon. Get Fisherman Ralph's number on Route 32 — if he calls about a Qwilfish outbreak, come back and catch one. Catch a SECOND Krabby for a later trade.
Enter Union Cave at the south end of Route 32. Sandshrew appears Morning/Day. The cave is bigger than it looks but you can't fully explore it yet; the far side leads to Azalea Town.
Go to Kurt's house in Azalea; he leaves for the Slowpoke Well. Follow and defeat the Rocket grunt leading the raid, then catch a Slowpoke. Afterward Kurt makes special balls from Apricorns.
Leave Azalea west (defeat your rival first) into Ilex Forest. Grab the Cut HM here. Paras appears any time; Psyduck, Oddish and Venonat at Night.

Even blocked off without Cut, you can Headbutt trees once Slowpoke learns Headbutt. Try a tree and see what falls out: Pineco in Ilex Forest, Aipom and Heracross in Azalea Town, Exeggcute back on Route 32.

chapters.title: Chapter 3 — Pre-Badge #3: Whitney
chapters.intro: Make sure you secure the Moon Stone from Mom this section (deposit up to P18,270). With Cut you push through Ilex Forest to Goldenrod City. Route 34, the Odd Egg, the Game Corner, the Bug-Catching Contest and a stash of evolution stones await. Target: 109 caught before Whitney.

items.text: Several new Pokémon plus the Day-Care. Snubbull appears Morning/Day, Drowzee at Night; everything else any time. Catch a SECOND Abra for an upcoming trade and get Picnicker Gina's number (she gives Leaf Stones).

Talk to the old man outside the Day-Care for the Odd Egg. SAVE BEFORE TALKING TO HIM — the species is fixed when received. It can be Pichu, Cleffa, Igglybuff, Tyrogue, Smoochum, Elekid or Magby. Soft-reset until you get Tyrogue (it nets four Pokémon now via the Hitmon line).
Breed your Jigglypuff with Ditto at the Day-Care to produce Igglybuff.

sections.heading: 3.3 · Goldenrod City — bike, Underground & Game Corner

items.text: The biggest city in Johto. Grab the bike. The Underground has a Coin Case for the Game Corner, and you can trade your spare Abra for a Machop. Crystal's Game Corner offers Cubone and Wobbuffet.
North of Goldenrod, Route 35 holds an ultra-rare 1% Yanma. Through the north gate is National Park: Nidoran♂/♀ Morning/Day, Sunkern daytime. On Tue/Thu/Sat the Bug-Catching Contest runs here.

Now evolve your stone Pokémon. Mom's Moon Stone evolves one of Jigglypuff / Nidorina / Nidorino; two Sun Stones come from winning the Bug Contest; Leaf and Fire Stones arrive via Gina and Schoolboy Alan's PokéGear calls (force them with the daylight-savings trick).

chapters.title: Chapter 4 — Pre-Badge #4: Morty
chapters.intro: Catch Sudowoodo, sweep Routes 37–43 and Olivine, pick up the Good Rod, then — yes, this early — chase down and catch the roaming beasts Raikou and Entei. Target: 142 caught before Morty.

items.text: After Whitney, return to Route 36 and talk to the lady by the 'tree'. She sends you to the Goldenrod flower shop for the SquirtBottle; bring it back and SAVE before using it on the tree — this is your only Sudowoodo. The man on its right then gives you the Rock Smash TM.
Route 37 has a single new catch, Stantler, at Night only.
Reach Ecruteak and enter the Pokémon Center; Bill heads back to Goldenrod. Follow him and he gives you an Eevee. Breed it five times — use the babies for Espeon/Umbreon (higher base happiness).

Take on the Kimono Girls for the Surf HM, then visit the Burned Tower. Koffing lurks on any floor. In the basement you disturb the three legendary beasts — Raikou and Entei begin roaming; Suicune appears in set locations later.
West to Route 38. Magnemite any time, Tauros & Miltank Morning/Day, Meowth at Night. Beat Lass Dana and get her number for Thunder Stones.
On Route 39 between Ecruteak and Olivine, stop at Moomoo Farm and help nurse the sick Miltank, Moomoo, back to health.
Reach the lighthouse top and talk to Jasmine to start Amphy's errand (not done yet). Trade your spare Krabby for a Voltorb, grab the Strength HM, then smash rocks on Route 40 for a chance at Shuckle.

sections.heading: 4.6 · Good Rod fishing & the Water Stone

items.text: Pick up the Good Rod in Olivine for more catches, then journey to Route 42 / Mt. Mortar. Defeat Fisherman Tully past the mountain for the final stone contact — Water Stones now evolve several Pokémon.
Rest in Mahogany Town, then head north to Route 43 for a Farfetch'd. Keep going to the Lake of Rage (nothing to do there yet).

Time to catch TWO legendaries early. Raikou and Entei roam Johto, moving each time you change area. Hunt from Ecruteak / Route 36 / Route 37 (no gates). Spray a Repel (lead ≤ Lv. 40), chip their HP across encounters, trap with Mean Look, status them, and use Ultra/Great Balls (the Fast Ball is bugged). Save between attempts.
As you cross Route 42 during all this, you'll witness Suicune in a set location — part of the scripted sequence that lets you catch it later.

chapters.title: Chapter 5 — Pre-Badge #5: Pryce
chapters.intro: Surf opens new ground: Tohjo Falls, the depths of Union Cave and the Ruins of Alph, Route 41 and Cianwood. Catch the red Gyarados, clear the Mahogany Rocket Hideout, then pick Pryce (he unlocks Whirlpool). Target: 150 caught.

items.text: Surf east from New Bark to Route 27 and enter Tohjo Falls to reach a Moon Stone. In Union Cave's basement, Surf across to a grassy bit of the Ruins of Alph for Natu and Smeargle; a Friday brings Lapras to the lowest floor. The Aerodactyl puzzle room hides a third Moon Stone (use Flash on the wall writing).

Surf south from Olivine over Route 40 onto Route 41 (borders Cianwood) for a Mantine. At Cianwood, grab Amphy's medicine at the pharmacy; head north to see Suicune, where Eusine challenges you — required to meet Suicune later. Mania here lends you the Shuckle 'Shuckie'.
As the Suicune sequence continues you'll also witness it on Route 36.

sections.heading: 5.3 · Lake of Rage & the Mahogany Rocket Hideout

items.text: Surf to the red Gyarados at the Lake of Rage. After catching/defeating it, Lance teams up with you to raid the Rocket Hideout in Mahogany. Inside you get the Whirlpool HM — also grab the Thief TM for a Moon Stone later. The Fishing Guru by the lake rewards a record Magikarp.
Three gyms are open now; Pryce is the pick because the Glacier Badge lets you use Whirlpool (and unlocks more Pokémon).

chapters.title: Chapter 6 — Pre-Badges #6 & #7: Jasmine & Chuck
chapters.intro: A very short stretch: Whirlpool opens the Whirl Islands for Seel and Horsea, then cure Amphy and clear both Chuck and Jasmine back to back. Target: 154 caught.

items.text: With Whirlpool, enter the Whirl Islands on Route 41. Seel appears in the caves except at Night; Horsea is found while Surfing.
Deliver the medicine to the top of the Olivine Lighthouse to cure Amphy — this opens Jasmine's gym. Defeat Chuck and Jasmine in a row.

chapters.title: Chapter 7 — Pre-Badge #8: Clair
chapters.intro: Stop Team Rocket's Radio Tower takeover in Goldenrod, then use the Clear Bell to catch Suicune at the Tin Tower. Cross Route 44 and the Ice Path to Blackthorn, fish two Dratini on Route 45, then beat Clair. Target: 170.

items.text: After the 7th badge Professor Elm calls you to the Goldenrod Radio Tower. Clear it of Team Rocket; you can train against the grunts.

Clearing the Radio Tower earns the Clear Bell. Go to the Tin Tower in Ecruteak, beat the sages, and follow the trail. SAVE BEFORE ENTERING — if you fail, Suicune is gone forever. Unlike the others it will NOT flee. It knows Rain Dance, Bubblebeam, Leer and Gust; bring a resist, drop its HP to red and inflict sleep/paralysis, then use Ultra Balls.

With 7 badges the man blocking eastern Mahogany is gone. Route 44's grass sits mid-water (Surf across): Lickitung Morning/Day, Tangela any time. The Ice Path holds the Waterfall HM, Swinub & Jynx Morning/Day, Delibird & Sneasel at Night (rates rise on lower floors).

Blackthorn has a required in-game trade: a female Dragonair for a Dodrio (available only after badge 8). South on Route 45 (bring Fly for the ledges): Gligar any time, Skarmory Morning/Day; fish the small pond on the right for two Dratini (one female). Then breed Dodrio→Doduo and Jynx→Smoochum at the Day-Care.

chapters.title: Chapter 8 — Pre-Elite Four
chapters.intro: Clear the Dragon's Den trial for badge 8 and Waterfall, grab the Master Ball from Elm, then cross into Route 27 and Victory Road catching the last Pokémon before challenging the Elite Four. Target: 174 caught.

items.text: After Clair, complete the trial in the Dragon's Den for your final badge and Waterfall. Answer every question of the Master's quiz correctly on the FIRST attempt to receive a Dratini with ExtremeSpeed. Then see Prof. Elm in New Bark Town for the Master Ball.

sections.heading: 8.2 · Into Kanto's edge — Route 27 & Victory Road

items.text: Surf east of New Bark to Route 27 (cross Tohjo Falls via Waterfall). Catch a Ponyta Morning/Day (more common on Route 26). In Victory Road, catch Rhyhorn Morning/Day — or hunt the 5% Rhydon directly.
At the Indigo Plateau your rival challenges you in an optional battle before the League. Then take on the Elite Four and Champion Lance.

chapters.title: Chapter 9 — Conquering Kanto
chapters.intro: As Champion you sail to Kanto. Most remaining Pokémon can be caught before a single Kanto badge — restore the Power Plant, upgrade the radio card, sweep the region, and catch Lugia and Ho-Oh. (Virtual Console players can also pick up Celebi.) Target: 203 caught.

items.text: VC ONLY: collect the GS Ball from the Goldenrod Pokémon Center upstairs, give it to Kurt in Azalea, return next day, then interact with the Ilex Forest shrine to battle a Lv. 30 Celebi (it Recovers — chip its HP and status it like Suicune). Cartridge players cannot get Celebi.

Sail the S.S. Aqua from Olivine to Vermilion. Go north to the Route 10 Power Plant area: catch an Electabuzz in the grass, then learn a part was stolen. Chase the Rocket through the Cerulean Gym, recover the part from Nugget Bridge, and return it to the Power Plant.

sections.heading: 9.3 · Rock Tunnel, the radio card & Magnet Train

items.text: South to Rock Tunnel: catch Kangaskhan (lower floors) and find wild Cubone/Marowak holding Thick Clubs. Exit south to Lavender Town's Radio Tower for the radio expansion card (PokéFlute). In Saffron, the Copycat wants her doll — fetch it from the Vermilion Pokémon Fan Club to get the Magnet Train pass.

From Saffron head toward Celadon. Route 7 at Night has Houndour and Murkrow. In Celadon, Surf the small pool for Grimer (10% Muk). The Game Corner sells Porygon (5,555 coins) and Larvitar (8,888). Prove to Eusine in the Celadon Mart that you've caught Raikou/Entei/Suicune for the Rainbow Wing. Slugma hides on Routes 16–18 in daytime.

On Route 15 catch the 1% Chansey (Lv. 25 — repel-trick with a Lv. 24 lead helps). Breed it so you keep one, then trade a Chansey on Route 14 for Aerodactyl. Bill's grandfather on Route 25 rewards showing him specific Pokémon.

Wake the Snorlax east of Vermilion with the PokéFlute (save first) to open Diglett's Cave for Diglett. Emerge on Route 2 for Pikachu, then Pewter (Silver Wing from the old man) and Mt. Moon for Clefairy and a Moon Stone (Monday-night ritual needs Rock Smash). Surf at Pallet to Route 21 for Mr. Mime.
Back at the Johto Day-Care, breed your Kanto catches for their babies. Daisy Oak in Pallet will comb a Pokémon for you.

With the Rainbow Wing and Silver Wing, catch BOTH Lugia (deep in the Whirl Islands) and Ho-Oh (top of the Tin Tower), each at Lv. 60. Heavy Balls help on Lugia; use your Master Ball on one and catch the other normally.

chapters.title: Chapter 10 — The Kanto Gym Circuit
chapters.intro: A POC catches everything before Kanto badges, so this chapter collects the eight Kanto gym wins and their Set-mode challenges in one place. Each badge is also a POC checkpoint at 203 Pokémon. Order follows the community route: Surge → Sabrina → Misty → Erika → Janine → Brock → Blaine → Blue.

chapters.title: Chapter 11 — Mt. Silver & Red
chapters.intro: With all 16 badges, Professor Oak opens Mt. Silver. Catch the last two wild Pokémon in Silver Cave, then face the former Champion, Red, at the summit. This completes the challenge: 206 caught (207 on VC with Celebi).

items.text: From the Pokémon League gates, exit west to Route 28 and the Pokémon Center at Silver Cave. On the entrance floor (Morning/Day) catch a Magmar to breed for Magby; Misdreavus appears on the next floor at Night.
Climb to the top of Mt. Silver to battle Red. His team is very high level — bring your best six.

chapters.title: Roundup — Collectible & Challenge Checklist
chapters.intro: These achievements span the whole game — work them in alongside the route. They cover full-region trainer/item sweeps, the complete Unown set, every trade and gift, the fishing rods, the Battle Tower, and the grand 'Gotta Catch 'Em All' capstone.
